title: Fix: Line of Business Update Disables Metric Navigation Toggle
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the Previous/Next metric navigation to continue working after I update a taxonomy value, so that I can efficiently edit multiple metrics in sequence without reopening the sidebar.
description:
Bug fix for an issue where updating the Line of Business or Topic taxonomy on a metric caused the Previous/Next navigation buttons in the sidebar to stop functioning. After the taxonomy update, clicking the arrows would not advance to the adjacent metric, and the UI remained locked on the current metric. Closing and reopening the sidebar restored normal behavior.

The root cause was that the taxonomy update event was interfering with the sidebar's metric navigation state.
acceptance_criteria: Previous/Next navigation continues to work after updating Line of Business on a metric
Previous/Next navigation continues to work after updating Topic on a metric
Sidebar data and main data table both update correctly when navigating after a taxonomy change
No need to close and reopen the sidebar to restore navigation
ux_changes: Fixes the sidebar Previous/Next navigation so it remains functional after a taxonomy (Line of Business or Topic) update. Navigation state is no longer reset by taxonomy change events.
implementation_notes: The taxonomy update handler was resetting the navigation state. Fix ensures the metric index tracking persists through taxonomy save events.
comment_summary: Developer acknowledged the issue and fixed it promptly. Pushed fix for review.
